LinkedIn data: profiles, people search,: LinkedIn data: profiles, people search, companies, and recent posts. Works keyless under a free daily trial, or with a paid API key for higher limits and realtime fetches.
Recent (default) serves cached data from: Recent (default) serves cached data from the last few hours when available; Realtime forces a live fetch for +2 credits (refunded if the live fetch falls back to cached data). Keyless calls only support Recent.
Spend ceiling for this one call. The cal: Spend ceiling for this one call. The call is rejected (and nothing is charged) if its quote exceeds this. 0 is valid: the error returns the quoted price of the call without running it.
Fetch a LinkedIn company page by URL, sl: Fetch a LinkedIn company page by URL, slug, or website domain. Costs 4 credits; a website domain is quoted at +4 credits for resolution, refunded at settlement when the domain is already known. Works with or without an API key.
Company URL (e.g. https://www.linkedin.c: Company URL (e.g. https://www.linkedin.com/company/microsoft), the slug after /company/ (e.g. microsoft), or a website domain (e.g. microsoft.com).
Search people on LinkedIn by keywords an: Search people on LinkedIn by keywords and filters. Costs 10 credits including the first 10 results; each further 10 results add 1 credit (max 30 per call; keyless and trial callers are capped at 10). Works with or without an API key.
Free-text query: a name, a title, or bot: Free-text query: a name, a title, or both.
Company name, slug, numeric id, or urn:l: Company name, slug, numeric id, or urn:li:fsd_company URN. Names are resolved automatically.
Same accepted forms as Current Company.
How many results to return (1-30). The f: How many results to return (1-30). The first 10 are included in the base price; each further 10 add 1 credit. Keyless and trial callers are capped at 10. With a company NAME filter keep this at 20 or less; use the company numeric id or URN for 30.
Cursor from a previous page. A cursor pa: Cursor from a previous page. A cursor page is a new call priced by its own limit, so prefer a larger limit over paginating.
Fetch a LinkedIn person profile by URL,: Fetch a LinkedIn person profile by URL, slug, or URN. Costs 4 credits; the first 2 profile sections are included, each extra section adds 2 credits. Works with or without an API key.
LinkedIn profile URL (e.g. https://www.l: LinkedIn profile URL (e.g. https://www.linkedin.com/in/williamhgates), the slug after /in/ (e.g. williamhgates), or a urn:li:fsd_profile URN.
Extra profile sections to include. The f: Extra profile sections to include. The first 2 are included in the base price; each section beyond 2 adds 2 credits (max 4).
Identify what a LinkedIn URL points at (: Identify what a LinkedIn URL points at (person, company, or post) and get its stable id, handle, and canonical URL. Costs 2 credits; works with or without an API key.
Identify a LinkedIn URL before fetching: Identify a LinkedIn URL before fetching it: returns type (person, company, or post), the stable id, handle, and canonical URL. Messy URLs with utm params, www/m subdomains, or trailing slashes are fine. Skip this when you already have a clean slug, URN, or URL — Get LinkedIn Profile and Get LinkedIn Company accept those directly, so resolving first would waste 2 credits. Non-LinkedIn URLs are rejected. Costs 2 credits. Works keyless. Read-only lookup, safe to repeat.
Any LinkedIn profile, company, or post U: Any LinkedIn profile, company, or post URL, e.g. https://www.linkedin.com/in/williamhgates or https://www.linkedin.com/company/microsoft.
Fetch the recent LinkedIn posts of a per: Fetch the recent LinkedIn posts of a person or company, one page per call with a cursor for older posts. Costs 4 credits per page. Works with or without an API key.
Person or company URL, slug, person URN,: Person or company URL, slug, person URN, or company website domain (e.g. microsoft.com).
Cursor from a previous page, for older p: Cursor from a previous page, for older posts.
\nOptional. Every action below also work:
Optional. Every action below also works with no key: calls run keyless under a free per-IP daily budget (20 credits/day, recent data only, no realtime fetches).

Paste an API key to raise those limits and unlock realtime fetches; it is sent as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Get a paid key at https://veezee.io/upgrade.
